data.description:
## GHA reconcile-bridge-canary: inject signing key + flip committer email to bot

The canary writes non-exempt tickets via the normal `rebar` CLI (`rebar create bug` /
`comment` / `transition` in `.github/workflows/reconcile-bridge-canary.yml`), so it will
SIGN AUTOMATICALLY through `_seam.append_event` once identity + key are configured — no
code change needed here (unlike the reconciler).

- Inject `secrets.REBAR_BOT_SIGNING_KEY` to a job-local file; set `identity.signing_key` to it.
- Set the committer email to `joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com` (from the current bridge default) so git-email resolution maps to the Rebar Bot identity.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `reconcile-bridge-canary.yml` injects the signing key securely and sets committer email to the bot.
- [ ] A canary-created bug/comment/transition is signed as Rebar Bot (verifiable via `verify-identity`).
- [ ] No code change required here is introduced; key never logged/committed.
data.title: GHA reconcile-bridge-canary: inject signing key + flip committer email to bot
